name: Approval Gate
summary: Route risky tool use through an explicit approval or policy check before execution.

sourceRepos.summary: Claude Code lifecycle hooks and skill metadata demonstrate permission gating before tool use.
Hook router patterns show structured pre-tool safety checks.
Codex hook registration and safety prompts provide cross-runtime gating provenance.

extraction.notes: Seeded from common pre-tool approval gates; extractor can later confirm hook file and manifest shapes.

applicability.whenToUse: Use when requested behavior needs human confirmation, policy checks, or elevated tool access.
risks: Too many gates can make the harness unusable; ask what actions truly require approval.
followUpQuestions: Which tools or file paths require approval?

name: Review Loop
summary: Run a reviewer/verifier pass after implementation and loop back for fixes when checks fail.

sourceRepos.summary: Specialized reviewer roles and QA cycling provide Codex loop provenance.
Ralph-style evaluation loop provides agentic review/retry semantics.

extraction.notes: Seeded from review and QA skills; future extractor should index skill descriptions and hook transitions.

applicability.whenToUse: Use when the harness must inspect outputs and automatically request repairs.
risks: Loop exit criteria must be explicit to avoid endless retries.
followUpQuestions: What proves the work is acceptable?

name: MCP Registration
summary: Register tools/resources through an MCP server boundary so the host runtime can call controlled capabilities.

sourceRepos.summary: OpenAgent provides the richest MCP registration and management reference.
Ouroboros exposes workflow operations through plugin/MCP-style boundaries.
Claude plugin manifest and hooks show installable runtime integration shape.

extraction.notes: Seeded from MCP directories/manifests; future extractor should read manifest server declarations.

applicability.whenToUse: Use when the generated harness needs callable tools, resources, or structured host integration.
risks: MCP servers expand attack surface; pair with approval gates for risky tools.
followUpQuestions: What tools should the MCP server expose?
Should transport be stdio or another supported mode?

name: State Persistence
summary: Persist session decisions, progress, and memory so future prompts can resume safely.

sourceRepos.summary: Workflow state and checkpointing are the strongest persistence reference.
Session recovery and continuation hooks show runtime lifecycle persistence.
OMX state/notepad/wiki patterns show file-backed continuation state.

extraction.notes: Seeded from state/checkpoint files; future extractor should classify store paths and update hooks.

applicability.whenToUse: Use when the harness must remember intent, decisions, progress, or verification results.
risks: State schema drift can break resumes; version stored state.
followUpQuestions: What should be remembered between sessions?

name: Retry Loop
summary: Evaluate a failed attempt, mutate the plan or graph, and retry under bounded conditions.

sourceRepos.summary: Seed/evaluate/mutate loops provide evolutionary retry provenance.
Ralph loop encodes persistent retry-until-complete orchestration.
Codex skills expose bounded completion and QA loops.

extraction.notes: Seeded from Ralph/Ouroboros loops; future extractor should parse stop conditions and scoring functions.

applicability.whenToUse: Use when failures should trigger a controlled second attempt instead of immediate stop.
risks: Retry loops need caps and explicit failure reporting.

name: Subagent Delegation
summary: Split work into specialized agent roles with clear ownership and verification contracts.

sourceRepos.summary: Role prompts and delegation rules provide Codex-native subagent design.
Claude Code agent catalog provides mature role specialization.
Role-based sprint and review methodology provide task-lane decomposition.

extraction.notes: Seeded from agent catalogs; future extractor should index role prompts and task ownership rules.

applicability.whenToUse: Use when requested work benefits from specialized reviewers, builders, or researchers.
risks: Delegation without ownership causes conflicts; require bounded write scopes.
followUpQuestions: Which work can safely run in parallel?
